title: System and methods for generating optimal post times for social networking sites
abstract: A system and methods are disclosed for determining the ideal times for a person, software client, or other entity to post a message to a social networking site. An ideal time is a time when the post will have a maximum impact, where impact is some measure of success as defined by the posting entity. To determine the ideal times, the posting patterns of individual users are aggregated in a weighted fashion, taking into consideration both the entity's desired impact and the likelihood that the user will be online and able to view the post within a specified time frame.
claims: 1. A method for generating an ideal post time for a user of a social networking site, the method comprising the steps of: acquiring at least one attribute of a plurality of followers that follow an activity of the user on the social networking site; collecting a timeline for each of the plurality of followers according to an available sequence of said plurality of followers' posts and times of those posts; computing a score for each of the plurality of followers based on user interests and the plurality of followers' at least one attribute.
